papers.title: Contextual Procurement Auctions with Bandit Learning
papers.titleZh: 基于上下文的采购拍卖与老虎机学习
papers.abstract: We study repeated contextual procurement auctions in which the platform must learn context-dependent product values from bandit feedback. We give an exactly truthful explore-then-commit mechanism with $\widetilde O((ng)^{1/3}T^{2/3})$ regret. We also give a frozen-payment UCB mechanism with a regret-incentive tradeoff: the near-UCB tuning attains \(\widetilde O(\sqrt{ngT})\) welfare regret, while for fixed \(n,g\) its total incentive error is \(\widetilde O(T^{3/4})\); the balanced tuning gives \(\widetilde O(T^{2/3})\) on both scales. Regret is measured as welfare loss relative to the full-information efficient allocation. We prove a matching lower bound for the frozen-payment regret-incentive tradeoff.

summarySections.backgroundAndQuestion: 平台在重复采购中需根据上下文决定向谁购买，但产品的真实价值未知，只能通过过往结果逐步学习。传统拍卖机制假设价值已知，而这里的学习过程与激励兼容存在冲突：投标者可能操纵报价来影响学习路径。问题在于如何设计既鼓励真实报价又能高效探索价值的机制，尤其是在电力调度、算力分配等现实场景中。
summarySections.modelAndSetup: 模型中有n个生产者，每个在不同上下文中提供产品，平台观察到的价值带有噪声。生产者有私有成本并提交固定报价，平台通过bandit反馈学习价值均值。目标是最大化社会福利，基准是知道真实价值时的最优分配。机制需在未知价值下进行自适应分配，并用支付规则激励真实报价，同时衡量福利损失（regret）与激励偏差。
summarySections.contributionsAndResults: 论文提出了两种机制：一是探索-然后承诺（ETC），完全分离学习与分配，实现精确激励兼容，但遗憾为O((ng)^{1/3}T^{2/3})；二是冻结支付UCB（F-UCB），在初期独立探索后冻结支付估计，后续仅更新分配，达到O(√ngT)福利遗憾，但激励误差为O(T^{3/4})，二者存在权衡。下界证明该权衡不可避免。
summarySections.methodsAndTechniques: ETC机制通过前M轮与报价无关的探索获取价值估计，之后采用经验VCG支付，确保激励兼容。F-UCB则冻结支付估计以切断报价对支付的影响，利用UCB进行高效分配学习。关键技术是将支付学习与分配学习解耦，并通过浓度不等式和稳定性分析控制后悔与激励误差，最终证明上下界匹配。
summarySections.limitationsAndReadingValue: ETC虽精确激励兼容但遗憾较高，F-UCB虽接近最优遗憾却只能近似激励兼容，且两者都依赖初期独立探索。机制未更新支付可能偏离长期最优。最值得读的是其对激励与学习权衡的清晰刻画、机制设计的解耦思想，以及紧的上下界分析，为动态机制中的学习与激励问题提供了基准框架。

papers.title: Slack and Budget Breaking in Threshold Team Production
papers.titleZh: 阈值团队生产中的松弛与预算突破
papers.abstract: A threshold system completes a public task only after $κ$ verifiable shares are publicly committed. If the honest schedule creates \( \Nstar=κ+Δ\) share opportunities by deadline $t^\star$, then $Δ$ shares are slack such that a coalition delays completion if and only if it withholds at least $Δ+1$ shares. The incentive problem is therefore to price the cheapest sabotage set. Agents receive a direct fee $f$ per committed share. A delaying coalition may also obtain delay value at most $L$, and may earn additional fee revenue during recovery after the deadline. Let $R_1^+$ be a pathwise upper bound on the coalition's incremental fee revenue in a recovery slot that completes the task, including any same-slot overshoot. The principal can post a nonnegative completion bounty that depends only on committed shares, uses no deposits or punishments, and expires if completion is late. The optimal rule is uniform, as if completion occurs by $t^\star$, every admissible horizon share receives $B/\Nstar$, otherwise no bounty is paid. Full participation is ex-post strongly delay proof exactly when \( (Δ+1)f+\frac{Δ+1}{\Nstar}B \ge L+R_1^+ . \) Equivalently, the exact worst-case budget is \( B^\star = \frac{\Nstar}{Δ+1} \bigl(L+R_1^+-(Δ+1)f\bigr)^+ . \) The bound is tight for every nonnegative completion measurable bounty, among the $\Nstar$ horizon shares, some $Δ+1$ receive total bounty at most $(Δ+1)B/\Nstar$, and withholding precisely those shares delays completion. The result applies to threshold signatures, data availability certification, coded dissemination, and generic $k$-of-$n$ completion tasks. We also isolate a separate limit, no transfer rule based only on completed shares can remove a final slot race in which a coalition has already observed enough pre-completion shares to act.

summarySections.backgroundAndQuestion: 为什么在分布式系统中完成一个公共任务会面临延迟风险？因为任务需要至少K个参与者提交有效份额才能完成，而个体只关心自己的收益。如果一些参与者联合起来扣留份额，就能推迟完成时间以获取额外利益，比如区块链中的最大可提取价值（MEV）。本文研究的就是这种共谋延迟攻击下的激励问题，特别是在存在冗余机会时，如何设计奖励机制来防止此类行为。
summarySections.modelAndSetup: 模型考虑了一个由n个代理和外部发起者组成的系统，时间被划分为时隙。每个时隙有一定数量的代理可提交份额，当累计提交的合法份额达到K时任务完成。若诚实路径下截止前共有N*=K+△个机会，则△称为松弛量。共谋可通过扣留其被选中的份额来延迟完成，仅当扣留超过△个时才会成功。代理获得固定费用f，而发起者可设置基于完成情况的非负赏金B，目标是使及时完成成为强均衡结果。
summarySections.contributionsAndResults: 论文证明了最优的完成赏金机制是‘及时团队赏金’TIMELY-TEAM：若按时完成，每个截止前的有效贡献者均分赏金B/N*，否则无赏金。此时完全参与是事后强延迟免疫的充要条件为(△+1)f + B/N* × (△+1) > L + R，其中L是延迟带来的最大可再分配收益，R是恢复阶段可能获得的额外费用上限。该预算界限是紧的，适用于多种阈值任务场景。
summarySections.methodsAndTechniques: 作者通过建立基本的扣留几何关系——延迟当且仅当扣留数超过松弛量△——将问题转化为对最小破坏集合的定价。利用对称化论证说明匿名机制不失一般性，并结合路径式分析与期望损失比较，得出最坏情况下无法改进的预算下界。关键在于识别出最小延迟攻击只需恰好扣留△+1个份额，从而将复杂动态博弈简化为静态成本收益权衡。
summarySections.limitationsAndReadingValue: 结果依赖于赏金仅基于已承诺份额且不使用押金或惩罚的设计，无法消除最后一时隙内因信息可见导致的竞争。此外，假设转移效用完全可分割可能在现实中受限。尽管如此，本文提供了清晰的激励设计原则：正松弛将单点故障转为协同破坏，而均匀奖励所有潜在贡献者是最优对策。值得阅读其对区块链数据可用性、门限签名等系统的机制构建启示。

papers.title: When do prophets profit in prediction markets?
papers.titleZh: 预言家何时能在预测市场中获利？
papers.abstract: Prediction markets aggregate dispersed beliefs into prices that act as probabilistic forecasts of uncertain events. Classical theory establishes a clean equivalence between forecasting accuracy and trading profit, but only for the specific automated market maker (AMM) design. However, the largest exchanges today are based on central limit order books in which informed forecasters routinely lose money while uninformed strategies can profit on simple heuristics. We resolve this discrepancy by establishing a formal equivalence between predictive accuracy and profitability. For any strictly proper scoring rule $S$, we exhibit a "proper" betting strategy that depends only on the forecaster's prediction $\mathbf{p}$ and the market price $\mathbf{q}$, and earns positive expected profit whenever $\mathbf{p}$ outperforms $\mathbf{q}$ under $S$ and the market has sufficient liquidity. Moreover, this proper betting is essentially the only strategy with such robust profitability guarantee. The proof rests on a decomposition of expected profit that strictly generalizes the classical AMM guarantee and also explains how strategies can profit without an accuracy edge. Empirically, across thousands of forecasts by AI models, proper betting is the only strategy that reliably converts accuracy into profit, and we further identify systematic forecasting personas and show how the optimal proper strategy varies across them. A month-long live deployment on Kalshi achieves $+80.33\%$ return on investment with a Sharpe ratio of $3.35$.

summarySections.backgroundAndQuestion: 预测市场本应奖励更准确的预报者，但现实中许多优于市场的预测却无法盈利。现有理论仅适用于自动做市商（AMM）设计，而主流平台采用订单簿机制，存在滑点、价差和流动性限制，导致准确性与收益脱钩。本文追问：在一般市场中，何种下注策略能稳健地将预测优势转化为利润？这触及信息激励、价格形成与交易执行的根本矛盾。
summarySections.modelAndSetup: 模型考虑一个有K个互斥结果的事件，预报者持有信念p，面对市场价格向量q。交易者通过提交头寸向量s参与市场，实际成交价受价格影响函数p(s)决定，产生成本并承受非负的流动性损失。预报者的预期利润由真实分布p*下的收益减去成本构成。核心设定不依赖特定市场机制，适用于AMM和中央限价订单簿（CLOB），允许价差、滑点和动态报价。
summarySections.contributionsAndResults: 论文提出‘适当下注’（proper betting）策略：对于任意严格真评分规则S及其势函数G，下注方向为∇G(p)−∇G(q)。该策略在流动性充足时，只要预报者在S下优于市场，就能保证正期望利润。进一步证明，这是唯一具有此稳健盈利能力的策略类别。实证显示，该策略在数千AI预测中显著胜出，并在真实部署中实现+80.33% ROI。
summarySections.methodsAndTechniques: 核心方法是将预期利润分解为三项：评分差距、Bregman散度与流动性损失。这一分解推广了经典AMM保证，并解释了为何无精度优势者仍可盈利（靠分歧项）。证明利用凸分析与势函数性质，建立评分规则与盈利策略的等价刻画。后续扩展至经验得分、价差环境与序列交易，均基于同一分解框架。
summarySections.limitationsAndReadingValue: 结果依赖预报者信念p与市场价格q的可获取性，且‘稳健盈利’关注存在性而非最优性。策略虽普适，但最优评分规则需依预报者类型（如保守/激进）调整。最值得读的是其统一视角：将评分规则从评估工具变为交易指令生成器，揭示了预测、机制与利润间的深层联系，对AI代理交易具直接指导价值。

papers.title: Network games with heterogeneous players
papers.titleZh: 具有异质参与者的网络博弈
papers.abstract: Real social and economic networks involve individuals with diverse incentives, yet most studies of network games assume homogeneous preferences or few player types. We introduce a general framework for binary choice network games with fully heterogeneous payoff structures. We first show that any such game can be transformed into an equivalent one with conformist, rebel, and stubborn archetypes, preserving equilibria and best response trajectories. We then establish sufficient conditions for pure strategy Nash equilibrium existence and convergence of best response dynamics on arbitrary networks, while proving that equilibria almost surely vanish in large sparse random networks. We further develop a deterministic approximation approach that predicts evolutionary trends and equilibrium strategy frequencies from network homophily and heterophily patterns, without computing equilibria explicitly. Extending the framework to limited information, we prove that dynamics converge either to a unique limited information equilibrium or to a unique stationary distribution, and we derive necessary and sufficient conditions for the existence of the limited information equilibrium. We validate our predictions using Prisoner's Dilemma games on real social networks that incorporate heterogeneous altruism and peer influence. These findings together provide a unified framework for equilibrium existence, evolutionary dynamics, and equilibrium outcome prediction in heterogeneous network games.

summarySections.backgroundAndQuestion: 现实中的社交与经济网络中个体激励多样，但多数网络博弈研究假设参与者偏好同质。本文指出，在完全异质收益结构下，纯策略纳什均衡的存在性与动态收敛性缺乏系统分析。作者旨在填补这一方法论空白，探究异质性与网络结构如何共同影响战略互动的稳定性。
summarySections.modelAndSetup: 模型考虑无向网络上的二元策略博弈，每个玩家拥有不同的收益矩阵，从而分为协调者、反协调者和顽固型三类。通过引入等价的三类玩家（从众者、叛逆者、顽固者）的简化模型，保留原博弈的均衡与最佳响应轨迹。玩家基于邻居策略进行异步最佳响应更新，研究其长期行为。
summarySections.contributionsAndResults: 论文建立了纯策略纳什均衡存在的充分条件，如网络中从众-叛逆边较少或某类玩家内部连接紧密时均衡存在；而在大型稀疏随机网络中均衡几乎必然消失。此外，提出了确定性常微分方程近似方法，无需显式计算即可预测策略频率演化趋势，并在有限信息下证明动态收敛至唯一均衡或平稳分布。
summarySections.methodsAndTechniques: 核心方法是将异质收益博弈转化为等价的三类玩家博弈，利用势博弈与局部势博弈工具分析均衡存在性。通过均值场与随机逼近，将离散动态简化为常微分方程系统，分析其固定点与全局动力学。在有限信息设定下，借助马尔可夫链收敛性与图着色思想，刻画唯一均衡的存在条件。
summarySections.limitationsAndReadingValue: 主要局限在于均衡存在性的条件仅为充分非必要，且高维网络的精确刻画仍具挑战。尽管如此，该框架统一处理了异质性下的均衡存在、动态演化与结果预测，其模型转化思想与确定性近似方法对研究复杂网络中的合作与冲突具有广泛阅读价值，尤其适合关注博弈学习与社会动态的研究者。

papers.title: Axioms for Correlated Equilibrium
papers.abstract: We characterize correlated equilibrium in finite normal-form games. Interpreting correlated strategies as action recommendations, we show that correlated equilibrium is the unique solution concept that never recommends a pure-strategy dominated action, treats payoff-equivalent actions interchangeably, and respects the sure-thing principle under uncertainty about payoffs and the correlation device. A parallel characterization identifies coarse correlated equilibrium among solution concepts that recommend dominant actions whenever they exist and treat payoff-equivalent actions as strongly interchangeable.

summarySections.backgroundAndQuestion: 为什么要研究相关均衡的公理基础？传统博弈论中，纳什均衡等解概念缺乏统一的选择标准。本文从公理化角度出发，追问哪些合理性原则能唯一确定相关均衡。它填补了已有工作多聚焦于独立策略的空白，试图通过一致性、后果主义和理性等行为假设，澄清相关均衡背后的决策逻辑，并为不同解概念提供选择依据。
summarySections.modelAndSetup: 作者如何形式化这一问题？模型固定玩家集合，在有限动作的规范式博弈中，将相关策略视为推荐装置生成的动作组合分布。解概念是从博弈到相关策略集的映射。核心公理包括：一致性（对支付不确定性的sure-thing原则）、（强）后果主义（对复制动作的不变性）和（弱）理性（不推荐被支配动作），并要求解概念连续且凸值。
summarySections.contributionsAndResults: 论文最关键的发现是什么？作者证明：满足一致性、后果主义和理性的唯一解概念是相关均衡；而若改用强后果主义和弱理性，则唯一解是粗糙相关均衡。这表明两种均衡分别对应不同的信息处理方式——前者要求玩家根据推荐更新信念，后者则允许忽略推荐信息直接承诺行动。
summarySections.methodsAndTechniques: 作者如何证明这些刻画结果？主要采用分解与重构策略：先证明所有纯纳什均衡必须被包含，再利用匹配便士等对称博弈结合凸组合和动作复制，通过一致性逐步扩展到一般博弈。关键技术是构造特定博弈序列，使违背公理会导致矛盾，从而锁定唯一可能的解概念。
summarySections.limitationsAndReadingValue: 这篇论文有何局限与阅读价值？结果依赖凸值性和连续性假设，且未解决连续性是否必要。尽管如此，其价值在于清晰揭示了不同公理组合如何导向不同均衡概念，特别适合想深入理解相关均衡本质、公理化方法在博弈论中的应用以及信息结构对激励兼容影响的读者。

papers.title: Graph Convolutional Attention: A Spectral Perspective on Graph Denoising and Diffusion
papers.titleZh: 图卷积注意力：图去噪与扩散的谱视角
papers.abstract: Denoising graphs is a fundamental problem in graph learning and the core operation of graph diffusion models. Attention-based architectures like graph transformers have recently shown promise in denoising graphs. However, our principled understanding of attention-based graph denoising remains limited, making it unclear whether standard attention is the right mechanism for this task. Here we show that, under a denoising objective, linear attention is suboptimal and can only learn an average spectral denoising filter over the training distribution. This creates a fundamental limitation as graphs often vary spectrally across the distribution. To overcome this limitation, we introduce Spectral Attention, which directly utilizes the input graph spectrum and provably outperforms linear attention by a margin governed by the spectral diversity of the distribution. We then derive Graph Convolutional Attention (GCA), a practical and permutation-equivariant realization of this idea that implements spectral denoising through graph-filtered queries and keys. For stochastic block models, GCA provably matches the idealized Spectral Attention mechanism. We further show that the softmax operation, that follows the attention, provides additional denoising by approximately projecting noisy eigenvectors onto the clean eigenspace. Empirically, replacing linear attention with GCA consistently improves graph denoising and diffusion on synthetic and real datasets, with gains strongly correlated with spectral diversity. In DiGress, GCA matches standard graph-transformer performance without computing expensive structural features, and when combined with the recently proposed PEARL positional encodings, avoids explicit eigendecomposition computations resulting in faster inference without degrading quality. The code can be found here: github.com/shervinkhalafi/graph_conv_att

summarySections.backgroundAndQuestion: 图去噪是图生成模型的核心任务，但现有基于注意力的图变换器缺乏理论指导。标准线性注意力只能学习图谱统计的平均规则，在谱特性差异大的图上表现受限。本文从谱分析角度出发，揭示了当前方法忽略输入图谱动态变化的根本缺陷，提出需要一种能自适应噪声图谱的注意力机制。
summarySections.modelAndSetup: 研究聚焦于从含噪邻接矩阵恢复干净图的监督去噪任务，输入为噪声图的特征向量，输出为重建图。通过合成与真实图数据集评估模型性能，使用重构误差和生成质量作为指标。特别设计了控制谱多样性的合成SBM数据，以验证理论预测。
summarySections.contributionsAndResults: 论文提出图卷积注意力（GCA），其性能严格优于线性注意力，且提升幅度与图分布的谱多样性正相关。在扩散模型中，GCA能替代昂贵的手工谱特征而不损失性能，并结合R-PEARL实现更快推理。实验证明了谱适应机制的有效性。
summarySections.methodsAndTechniques: GCA通过图滤波的查询与键实现谱感知注意力，使模型能根据噪声图的特征值动态调整滤波器。理论证明在随机块模型下，GCA可逼近最优谱注意力。后续的softmax操作进一步近似投影噪声特征向量至干净子空间，提供额外去噪效果。
summarySections.limitationsAndReadingValue: 理论分析主要基于渐近的大规模随机块模型，对一般图的适用性有待验证。实验未进行广泛超参调优，结论更多体现趋势而非绝对性能。最值得阅读的是其将谱分析与注意力机制结合的理论框架，为图生成模型设计提供了新原则。

papers.title: Provable learning separation for predicting time-evolution of quantum many-body systems
papers.titleZh: 预测量子多体系统时间演化的可证明学习分离
papers.abstract: Given that quantum computers are naturally suited to simulate the behavior of quantum many-body systems, an immediate question arises: can one formulate physically motivated quantum machine learning (QML) tasks that exhibit learning separations? We address this problem by studying the learnability of quantum many-body dynamics from the perspective of probably approximately correct (PAC)-learning. Concretely, we devise a supervised learning problem where the training set consists of specifications of randomized stabilizer probe states, evolution times sampled uniformly from a polynomially large time interval $[0,T]$, coupled with expectation values of certain observables evaluated on the resulting time-evolved state under an unknown Hamiltonian. For this learning task, we provide an efficient quantum procedure whose training phase learns the underlying Hamiltonian from short-time training samples, and whose deployment phase combines Hamiltonian simulation with the classical shadows protocol to perform inference on a newly given data point. By contrast, the existence of $O(\mathsf{poly}(n))$-time instances ensures classical hardness: by embedding a $\mathsf{BQP}$-complete computation into the polynomially long time-dynamics of a low-intersection variant of the Feynman-Kitaev clock Hamiltonian construction, we show that, for a certain family of input distributions, no randomized classical polynomial-time algorithm can fulfill our learning condition, unless $\mathsf{BQP}\subseteq\mathsf{P/poly}$. Furthermore, we show that the classically hard instance maintains quantum learnability. We also give an interpretation of our results in learning-assisted certified quantum simulation. Taken together, our results demonstrate a rigorous learning separation for a natural ML task based on Hamiltonian evolution, while building connections between quantum learning theory, quantum simulation, and QML.

summarySections.backgroundAndQuestion: 为什么在预测量子系统演化时，经典机器学习难以超越量子方法？本文探讨一个核心问题：是否存在物理上合理的学习任务，使得量子学习器能高效学会而任何经典算法都无法做到。尽管已有工作提出学习分离，但大多依赖密码学假设或受限于不现实的哈密顿量形式。本文旨在基于更自然的物理设定，建立严格的复杂性理论基础。
summarySections.modelAndSetup: 论文研究的任务是从随机初态和演化时间下测量得到的期望值中，预测未知哈密顿量驱动的时间演化结果。输入是探针态、演化时间和可观测量，输出是一组期望值。训练数据由短时演化样本构成，用于学习哈密顿量；测试则涵盖长时演化，可能编码BQP完全计算。评估采用PAC学习框架，要求在多项式时间内达到高精度泛化性能。
summarySections.contributionsAndResults: 本文首次证明了一类物理动机明确的量子学习任务存在指数级学习分离。作者构造了一个低交集哈密顿量家族，其长时间演化可实现BQP完全计算，从而确保经典学习不可能，除非BQP ⊆ P/poly。同时，他们设计了一个高效的量子学习算法，在短时数据上完成哈密顿量学习，并结合经典影子进行推理，实现了可证的量子优势。
summarySections.methodsAndTechniques: 方法分为两阶段：训练阶段利用短时演化数据，通过简化Haah等人提出的哈密顿量学习协议，以经典后处理恢复哈密顿量参数；推理阶段使用量子计算机模拟该哈密顿量并结合经典影子协议估计可观测量期望值。关键技术在于将时间平均积分转化为蒙特卡洛估计，并利用尾部界控制误差传播，使整个流程可在多项式时间内完成。
summarySections.limitationsAndReadingValue: 局限在于训练数据需提供向量值标签（多个可观测量），这比单标量更丰富，导致识别过程本身是经典的，分离源于后续的评估难度而非识别难度。此外，构造依赖特定哈密顿量结构。最值得读的是其问题设定与复杂性论证，为物理启发的量子学习优势提供了迄今最强有力的理论支持。

papers.title: ExplAIner: A Declarative Query Language for Explaining Classification Models
papers.titleZh: ExPLAINER: 一种用于解释分类模型的声明式查询语言
papers.abstract: The XAI community has studied a wide range of queries and scores for explaining predictions of ML models. From a data management perspective, this proliferation of explanation notions calls for declarative query languages in which such notions can be specified, combined, and analyzed uniformly. In this paper, we develop such a framework for Boolean models. We first revisit FOIL, an interpretability query language for black-box models, and show that it has two fundamental limitations: it cannot express central optimality-based explanation queries, and its evaluation problem over decision trees is hard for every level of the polynomial hierarchy. We then introduce ExplAIner, a query language based on FOIL with an extended vocabulary and a layered structure. We show that ExplAIner can express a broad family of explanation notions, including abductive, contrastive, feature-based, and distance-based queries. We also prove that the evaluation problem for each query in ExplAIner belongs to the Boolean hierarchy over every class of Boolean models for which some basic predicates can be evaluated in polynomial time. In particular, that property holds for deterministic and decomposable Boolean circuits. Finally, we introduce Opt-FOIL, an optimization-oriented fragment of ExplAIner for computing explanations that are minimal with respect to strict partial orders, and prove that its evaluation problem is in $\mathrm{FP}^{\mathrm{NP}}$ under the same tractability assumptions. These complexity results have a direct algorithmic consequence: a fixed ExplAIner query can be evaluated with a fixed number of calls to a SAT solver, while a notion of explanation specified in Opt-FOIL can be computed with a polynomial number of such calls. This is particularly relevant in formal XAI, where SAT solvers have been successfully used to compute explanations for several classes of ML models.

summarySections.backgroundAndQuestion: 当前可解释AI（XAI）提出了大量不同的解释定义，如反事实、特征重要性等，但缺乏统一的语言来表达和比较这些概念。直接为每个任务设计专用算法效率低下，且难以组合多种解释需求。本文从数据库视角出发，指出需要一个声明式查询语言，使用户只需说明‘想要什么样的解释’，而非‘如何计算’，从而实现解释任务的标准化与集成。
summarySections.modelAndSetup: 论文研究的是对布尔分类模型进行解释的问题，输入是一个模型和一个待解释实例，输出是满足特定逻辑条件的部分实例（即解释）。作者将模型视为黑箱函数，通过定义子实例间的蕴含关系与完整性谓词来构建语义结构，并在决策树、布尔电路等模型上评估查询的表达能力与计算复杂性，核心评价标准是查询能否在可控复杂度内被判定。
summarySections.contributionsAndResults: 论文提出了ExPLAINER语言及其优化片段OPT-FOIL，首次实现了对最小/最大解释等关键概念的统一表达。结果表明，ExPLAINER的查询可在布尔层次内高效评估，而OPT-FOIL能在多项式时间内通过调用SAT求解器完成最优解释的构造。这证明了声明式方法不仅能涵盖主流解释形式，还能提供清晰的计算边界，显著优于原有语言FOIL。
summarySections.methodsAndTechniques: 作者基于FOIL语言扩展出ExPLAINER，引入了按特征数排序的比较关系，使其能表达基数优化目标。语言采用分层结构：原子层处理语法关系，量化层结合模型行为谓词，顶层支持布尔组合。OPT-FOIL则进一步加入最小化算子，允许在任意偏序下搜索最优解释。其有效性源于将复杂查询转化为固定次数的NP预言机调用，契合现代SAT求解器的能力。
summarySections.limitationsAndReadingValue: 论文聚焦于布尔模型，未涉及连续特征或多类别输出，限制了现实场景的直接应用。此外，虽然理论上复杂度可控，但实际性能依赖底层SAT求解器效率。尽管如此，该工作的形式化框架和语言设计极具启发性，最值得阅读的是其对‘解释作为查询’的抽象思路、分层语言构造及复杂性分析，为构建系统化的XAI工具提供了坚实基础。

papers.title: When do prophets profit in prediction markets?
papers.titleZh: 预言家何时能在预测市场中获利？
papers.abstract: Prediction markets aggregate dispersed beliefs into prices that act as probabilistic forecasts of uncertain events. Classical theory establishes a clean equivalence between forecasting accuracy and trading profit, but only for the specific automated market maker (AMM) design. However, the largest exchanges today are based on central limit order books in which informed forecasters routinely lose money while uninformed strategies can profit on simple heuristics. We resolve this discrepancy by establishing a formal equivalence between predictive accuracy and profitability. For any strictly proper scoring rule $S$, we exhibit a "proper" betting strategy that depends only on the forecaster's prediction $\mathbf{p}$ and the market price $\mathbf{q}$, and earns positive expected profit whenever $\mathbf{p}$ outperforms $\mathbf{q}$ under $S$ and the market has sufficient liquidity. Moreover, this proper betting is essentially the only strategy with such robust profitability guarantee. The proof rests on a decomposition of expected profit that strictly generalizes the classical AMM guarantee and also explains how strategies can profit without an accuracy edge. Empirically, across thousands of forecasts by AI models, proper betting is the only strategy that reliably converts accuracy into profit, and we further identify systematic forecasting personas and show how the optimal proper strategy varies across them. A month-long live deployment on Kalshi achieves $+80.33\%$ return on investment with a Sharpe ratio of $3.35$.

summarySections.backgroundAndQuestion: 预测市场通过价格聚合分散信念，传统理论认为预测越准收益越高，但这仅适用于自动做市商（AMM）设计。现实中主流平台采用订单簿机制，存在滑点、价差和流动性限制，导致准确预测者常亏损，而不准的策略反而可能盈利。这暴露出一个核心问题：在一般市场中，预测准确性与交易利润之间缺乏可靠联系，如何设计一种稳健的下注策略来弥合这一鸿沟。
summarySections.modelAndSetup: 论文研究在任意价格影响函数下的预测市场中，如何将一个优于市场价格的预测转化为实际利润。输入是预测者的概率估计p和市场价q，输出是持仓向量s。作者定义‘稳健盈利’为只要预测在某种严格真得分规则下优于市场，策略就应获得正期望收益。实验基于数千个AI模型在Kalshi市场的历史预测，并进行了为期一个月的真实资金部署以验证效果。
summarySections.contributionsAndResults: 论文提出了‘恰当下注’策略，证明其在任何具备足够流动性的市场中都能将预测优势转化为正收益，且这是唯一一类具有此性质的策略。该策略统一并推广了AMM中的经典结果。实验证明，在回测中唯有恰当下注能稳定产生正回报，真实部署实现了80.33%的收益率和3.35的夏普比率，强有力支持了理论主张。
summarySections.methodsAndTechniques: 方法核心是为每个严格真得分规则S构造对应的‘恰当下注’策略，形式为s=∇G(p)−∇G(q)，其中G是S的凸势函数。其期望利润可分解为三项：得分差距、Bregman散度与流动性损失。前两项非负，当市场流动性足够高时总和超过第三项，从而保证盈利。反向证明则利用几何论证说明任何偏离该形式的策略都无法稳健盈利。
summarySections.limitationsAndReadingValue: 结论依赖于市场具备足够流动性以使Bregman散度抵消滑点，极端薄的市场可能不成立。此外，最优策略的选择取决于预测者的‘人设’特征，需事先识别。尽管如此，论文建立了预测质量与经济收益之间的根本联系。最值得读的是其理论框架、利润分解思想及对AI代理实战表现的系统分析，为构建高效信息聚合机制提供了坚实基础。

papers.title: Harnessing Code Agents for Automatic Software Verification
papers.titleZh: 利用代码智能体实现自动软件验证
papers.abstract: Formal verification offers the strongest guarantee of software correctness, but it does not scale: the proofs demanded by interactive theorem provers such as Coq require enormous expert effort. Large language models (LLMs) promise to generate these proofs automatically, yet existing approaches wire a fixed, human-designed proof strategy into the system and constrain the model to follow it (retrieving premises and predicting tactics one step at a time, or splitting goals by divide-and-conquer), and still prove only a fraction of their target theorems. We show that imposing such a strategy is unnecessary and limiting. Handing the whole lemma to a general LLM code agent (for example, Claude Code), free to choose its own approach, and wrapping it in a verification harness is both simpler and more effective, achieving full coverage: every targeted lemma proved, with no failures and no Coq expert intervention. The agent writes the proofs under feedback and hard constraints from the harness that keep each one sound (accepted only when the prover's kernel closes it), complete (no obligation left unproved or silently dropped), and terminating (no divergent tactics). We evaluate this harness plus code agent along three dimensions. (1) Core logic: on Iris, the state-of-the-art separation logic for concurrent and memory-manipulating programs, Aria proves all 4,257 lemmas of the four core modules and the 217 lemmas verifying Rust's standard libraries built on it, fully automatically. (2) Comparison with prior LLM provers: on reglang, where prior provers manage barely one in eight, Aria proves all 318. (3) Generality: on iris-lean, the unfinished Lean 4 port of Iris, it proves 72 not-yet-ported lemmas, showing the approach is not specific to Coq. A state-of-the-art model (Claude Opus 4.7) can write proofs for verified software development fully and automatically.

summarySections.backgroundAndQuestion: 形式化验证能提供最强的软件正确性保障，但依赖专家手动编写证明，成本极高，尤其在处理并发和共享内存的复杂程序时。现有基于大语言模型（LLM）的自动证明方法受限于固定的人工设计策略，如逐战术预测或分治拆解，导致覆盖率低，难以应对高阶并发分离逻辑等硬核场景。
summarySections.modelAndSetup: 论文研究如何让通用代码智能体全自动完成Coq中的定理证明任务。输入是待证引理及其上下文，输出是完整的证明脚本。系统通过一个验证外壳驱动智能体进行多轮尝试，每次失败后反馈精确错误信息。评估涵盖Iris核心库、RustBelt安全证明、正则语言库reglang及Lean版本Iris，指标为完全证明率、重试次数和耗时。
summarySections.contributionsAndResults: 论文首次实现了对专家级并发分离逻辑库Iris全部4257个引理的全自动无错证明，零人工干预。在Rust标准库验证和reglang上也达到100%覆盖率，远超先前方法。结果表明，无需预设证明策略，仅靠强LLM与闭环验证即可攻克最难的软件验证任务，颠覆了传统自动化证明的设计思路。
summarySections.methodsAndTechniques: 方法核心是将通用代码智能体（如Claude Code）与验证外壳结合。智能体自由决定证明路径，外壳则强制执行正确性、完整性和终止性约束，并通过内嵌反馈循环引导重试。关键创新在于Harness Hook Language（HHL），它将验证策略声明式编码，使整个流程可复用、可审计，摆脱了对特定搜索或分解机制的依赖。
summarySections.limitationsAndReadingValue: 尽管结果惊人，但高度依赖顶级闭源模型（如Claude Opus 4.7），开源模型在长证明上表现不佳。此外，实验集中于特定逻辑体系，通用性有待检验。最值得读的是其系统设计理念——如何用简单机制释放LLM潜力，以及HHL如何将安全与风格约束工程化，这对构建可信AI编程系统极具启发。
